questions.q: The term 'Bollywood' is a portmanteau combining 'Hollywood' with which Indian city's former name?
questions.explanation: 'Bollywood' blends 'Bombay' (the former name of Mumbai) with 'Hollywood'.

questions.q: In which Indian city is the film industry known as 'Bollywood' primarily based?
questions.explanation: Bollywood refers to the Hindi-language film industry centred in Mumbai (formerly Bombay).

questions.q: Which language are the films of 'Bollywood' primarily made in?
questions.explanation: 'Bollywood' denotes the Hindi-language film industry of Mumbai.

questions.q: The term 'Bollywood' came into common use from roughly which decade onwards?
questions.explanation: The label 'Bollywood' came into common use from around the 1970s onward.

questions.q: The first syllable 'B' in the word 'Bollywood' is taken from which of these?
questions.explanation: 'Bollywood' blends 'Bombay' and 'Hollywood'; the 'B' comes from Bombay, not Bengal, Bangalore or Bharat.

questions.q: The Bollywood-style nickname 'Tollywood' is most commonly applied to film industries in which language(s)?
questions.explanation: 'Tollywood' is used for the Telugu industry (Hyderabad) and the Bengali industry (named after Kolkata's Tollygunge).

questions.q: Which Bollywood-style nickname refers to the Tamil-language film industry?
questions.explanation: 'Kollywood' denotes the Tamil film industry, named after the Kodambakkam neighbourhood of Chennai.

questions.q: 'Bollywood' is best described as a nickname for which kind of entity?
questions.explanation: 'Bollywood' is an informal nickname for the Hindi-language film industry, not a studio, award, or government body.

questions.q: Songs and elaborate song-and-dance sequences are a hallmark especially associated with which Indian film tradition popularly called by a Hollywood-derived nickname?
questions.explanation: Bollywood (Hindi cinema) is internationally known for its elaborate musical song-and-dance sequences.

questions.q: Many practitioners criticise the term 'Bollywood' primarily because they regard it as what?
questions.explanation: Many film practitioners dislike 'Bollywood' because they see it as derivative of and subordinate to Hollywood.

questions.q: Mumbai, the home of Bollywood, was officially known by which name until it was renamed in 1995?
questions.explanation: The city was officially called Bombay until it was renamed Mumbai in 1995, though 'Bollywood' retained the older 'Bom-' root.

questions.q: Which of these is the popular nickname for the Malayalam-language film industry, formed in imitation of 'Bollywood'?
questions.explanation: 'Mollywood' is the nickname for the Malayalam film industry, after the 'M' of Malayalam.

questions.q: The Kannada-language film industry, another industry given a Hollywood-style nickname, is popularly called what?
questions.explanation: The Kannada film industry is nicknamed 'Sandalwood', referencing Karnataka's association with sandalwood.

questions.q: The 'Tolly-' in 'Tollywood', as applied to the Bengali film industry, is derived from which Kolkata locality?
questions.explanation: 'Tollywood' for Bengali cinema comes from Tollygunge, the Kolkata neighbourhood that was an early hub of Bengali film studios.

questions.q: Which Bollywood film, released in 1975, is frequently cited as one of the most iconic Hindi 'masala' blockbusters of all time?
questions.explanation: Sholay (1975), directed by Ramesh Sippy, is one of the most celebrated and enduring blockbusters of Hindi cinema.

questions.q: The Bollywood actor often referred to as the 'Shahenshah' and a dominant 'angry young man' star of 1970s Hindi cinema is:
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan rose to fame as the 'angry young man' of 1970s Bollywood and is nicknamed 'Shahenshah'.

questions.q: Which 1995 Bollywood romance, directed by Aditya Chopra, became famous for an exceptionally long continuous theatrical run in Mumbai?
questions.explanation: Dilwale Dulhania Le Jayenge (1995) ran for decades at Mumbai's Maratha Mandir cinema, a record for Indian cinema.

questions.q: In Bollywood film production, the singers who record songs that on-screen actors lip-sync to are known as what?
questions.explanation: Bollywood relies heavily on 'playback singers', whose recorded songs are lip-synced by actors on screen.

questions.q: Which Bollywood music director and composer scored films such as Roja and later won two Academy Awards for the non-Hindi film Slumdog Millionaire?
questions.explanation: A. R. Rahman, prominent in Bollywood and Tamil cinema, won two Oscars for Slumdog Millionaire (2008).

questions.q: Hindi cinema, popularly called Bollywood, is sometimes traced to its first sound film of 1931. Which film was that?
questions.explanation: Alam Ara (1931) was India's first sound feature, a milestone for what later became known as Bollywood.

questions.q: Which family-run banner, founded by Yash Chopra, is one of the most prominent production houses associated with Bollywood romance films?
questions.explanation: Yash Raj Films, founded by Yash Chopra in 1970, is a leading Bollywood production and distribution house.

questions.q: The three actors Shah Rukh Khan, Salman Khan and Aamir Khan are collectively known in Bollywood by what informal nickname?
questions.explanation: Shah Rukh, Salman and Aamir Khan are popularly dubbed the 'Three Khans' of Bollywood.

questions.q: The coinage of 'Bollywood' in the 1970s is often credited to writers in which field?
questions.explanation: The term is generally credited to film journalists and the popular press of the 1970s rather than the industry itself.

questions.q: Before 'Bollywood' became standard, the older Hollywood-style nickname 'Tollywood' was first applied in the 1930s to which industry?
options: The Bengali industry of Tollygunge, Calcutta
questions.explanation: 'Tollywood' was coined in the 1930s for the Bengali film industry centred in Tollygunge, predating 'Bollywood'.

questions.q: Strictly speaking, 'Bollywood' refers only to Hindi cinema and is NOT a synonym for which broader concept?
questions.explanation: Bollywood is only the Hindi-language industry; Indian cinema as a whole also includes Tamil, Telugu, Bengali, Malayalam and other industries.

questions.q: The Bollywood masala film, a genre mixing multiple modes, characteristically blends action, romance, comedy and which other key element?
questions.explanation: The 'masala' formula mixes action, romance and comedy with song-and-dance musical sequences in a single film.

questions.q: Which classic Bollywood epic, released in 1960 and directed by K. Asif, was a landmark big-budget historical romance famous for the song 'Pyar Kiya To Darna Kya'?
questions.explanation: Mughal-e-Azam (1960), directed by K. Asif, is a landmark Bollywood historical epic featuring 'Pyar Kiya To Darna Kya'.

questions.q: The 1957 Bollywood film Mother India, a major early international success for Hindi cinema, was directed by whom?
questions.explanation: Mother India (1957) was directed by Mehboob Khan and was nominated for the Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film.

questions.q: Which Bollywood actor of the 1950s and 1960s, known for the screen persona of a Chaplinesque 'tramp', directed and starred in films like Awaara and Shree 420?
questions.explanation: Raj Kapoor played a Chaplin-inspired tramp and directed and starred in Awaara (1951) and Shree 420 (1955).

questions.q: In terms of the sheer number of feature films produced annually, the Indian film industries (including Bollywood) collectively are generally described as which?
options: The smallest film output in Asia
Among the largest in the world by output
Limited to fewer than fifty films a year
questions.explanation: India, including Bollywood and the regional industries, is among the world's largest film producers by number of films released each year.
